title: Special Equations and Classical Models
subtitle: Singular solutions, classical curve problems, and the modeling tradition from fluids to ecology and economics.
description: Singular solutions, classical curve problems, and the modeling tradition from fluids to ecology and economics.

atAGlance.title: Unit 8 at a Glance: Core Sub-Topics
atAGlance.summary: Unit 8 moves from the algebraic theory of first-order ODEs into the classical applied models that drive physics, biology, and economics. It opens with two \emph{special equation types}---the Clairaut equation (singular-solution envelopes) and pursuit/tractrix curves---before building three major applied models: the Navier--Stokes-derived plane Poiseuille velocity profile, the supply-and-demand price-adjustment ODE, and the Lotka--Volterra predator-prey and competition systems. The unit closes with a structured survey of first-order solution methods.

bullets.lead: 8.1 The Clairaut Equation and Singular Solutions
bullets.body: general solution $y = Cx + f(C)$; singular (envelope) solution from the system $\{y = xp + f(p),\; 0 = x + f'(p)\}$.
tractrix DE $dy/dx = -\sqrt{a^2 - x^2}/x$; general pursuit $x\,y'' = k\sqrt{1+(y')^2}$ with speed ratio $k = v_m/v_d$.
Newtonian shear $\tau = \mu\,du/dy$; plane Poiseuille flow $\mu u'' = -G$ with no-slip boundary conditions.

bullets.lead: 8.4 Economic Models, Supply, Demand, and Price
bullets.body: price ODE $dp/dt = k(D - S)$; equilibrium $p^* = (a+c)/(b+d)$; convergence for $k(b+d) > 0$.
coexistence equilibrium $(c/d,\, a/b)$; conserved quantity $H = dx - c\ln x + by - a\ln y$.
two-species carrying-capacity system; nullcline inequalities for stable coexistence versus competitive exclusion.
decision tree: separable, linear (integrating factor), Bernoulli, exact; classify by structure before solving.

modules.title: The Clairaut Equation and Singular Solutions
modules.topics: A \emph{Clairaut equation} has the special form $y = xy' + f(y')$. Setting $p = y'$ and differentiating both sides with respect to $x$ yields $(x + f'(p))\,dp/dx = 0$, splitting into two branches whose solutions are the general family of straight lines and the singular envelope.

formulas.title: Clairaut Equation: General and Singular Solutions
formulas.body:
For $y = xp + f(p)$ with $p = y'$:
\[
  \strong{General solution:}\quad y = Cx + f(C) \quad (p = C = \text{const}),
\]
\[
  \strong{Singular solution (envelope):}\quad
  \begin{cases}
    y = xp + f(p) \\
    0 = x + f'(p)
  \end{cases}
\]
Eliminate $p$ from this system to obtain the envelope: a curve tangent to every
line in the general family.
The singular solution satisfies the ODE but \emph{cannot} be obtained from the
general solution by any choice of $C$.

methodology.title: Envelope Elimination Procedure
steps: Write the equation as $F(x,y,C) = y - Cx - f(C) = 0$.
Differentiate with respect to $C$: $\partial F/\partial C = -x - f'(C) = 0$, giving $x = -f'(C)$.
Solve for $C = C(x)$ and substitute back into $F = 0$ to obtain the envelope curve $y = y(x)$.
Verify: the envelope must satisfy the original ODE directly.

warning.title: Common Pitfall: Singular Solution Is Not a Particular Solution

modules.title: Pursuit Curves and the Tractrix
modules.topics: A \emph{pursuit curve} traces the path of a pursuer who always aims directly at a moving target. The tractrix is the classical limiting case in which the pursuer is dragged at constant distance $a$ from a target walking along a line.

formulas.title: Tractrix Differential Equation and Solution
formulas.body:
Target on the $y$-axis, pursuer starts at $(a,0)$, equal speeds, string length $a$:
\[
  \frac{dy}{dx} = -\frac{\sqrt{a^2 - x^2}}{x}, \qquad 0 < x \le a,\quad y(a) = 0.
\]
Solution (via $x = a\sin\theta$):
\[
  y(x) = a\ln\!\left(\frac{a + \sqrt{a^2 - x^2}}{x}\right) - \sqrt{a^2 - x^2}.
\]

formulas.title: General Pursuit Equation
formulas.body:
Dog at origin pursues owner on line $x = c$ at speed $v_m$; dog speed $v_d > v_m$;
$k = v_m/v_d < 1$:
\[
  x\,y'' = k\sqrt{1+(y')^2}, \qquad y(c) = 0,\quad y'(c) = 0.
\]
Solution ($k \ne 1$):
\[
  y(x) = \frac{1}{2}\!\left[\frac{x^{k+1}}{(k+1)c^k}
  - \frac{c^k x^{1-k}}{1-k}\right] + \frac{ck}{1-k^2}.
\]
Capture height: $y(0) = ck/(1-k^2)$.

warning.title: Common Pitfall: Direction of the Derivative Sign
warning.body:
In the tractrix, $dy/dx$ is \emph{negative} because $x$ decreases as the pursuer
is dragged toward the $y$-axis. Always verify the geometric sign from the
tangent-segment picture before writing the ODE.

modules.title: Fluid Dynamics and Viscosity
modules.topics: Newtonian viscosity: $\tau = \mu\,du/dy$. Plane Poiseuille flow reduces the momentum balance to a second-order constant-coefficient ODE with a parabolic solution.

formulas.title: Plane Poiseuille Velocity Profile
formulas.body:
$\mu\,u''(y) = -G$, $u(0) = u(h) = 0$:
\[
  u(y) = \frac{G}{2\mu}\,y(h - y), \qquad
  y_{\max} = \frac{h}{2}, \qquad
  u_{\max} = \frac{Gh^2}{8\mu}.
\]
Shear stress: $\tau(y) = \tfrac{G}{2}(h - 2y)$, linear, zero at the centerline.

formulas.title: 2D Incompressible Continuity and Stream Function
formulas.body:
$\partial u/\partial x + \partial v/\partial y = 0$.
Given $u$: integrate $\partial v/\partial y = -\partial u/\partial x$ in $y$.
Stream function: $u = \partial\psi/\partial y$, $v = -\partial\psi/\partial x$;
streamlines are $\psi(x,y) = C$.

warning.title: Common Pitfall: Both No-Slip Conditions Are Required
warning.body:
The Poiseuille ODE produces a quadratic with two free constants. Both
$u(0) = 0$ and $u(h) = 0$ must be applied; using only one gives a wrong family.

modules.title: Economic Models, Supply, Demand, and Price
modules.topics: Price adjusts at a rate proportional to excess demand. With linear demand and supply the ODE is first-order linear, and the equilibrium price is globally stable.

formulas.title: Price Adjustment ODE and Equilibrium
formulas.body:
$D = a - bp$, $S = -c + dp$ ($a,b,c,d > 0$):
\[
  \frac{dp}{dt} = k\bigl[(a+c) - (b+d)p\bigr],
  \qquad
  p(t) = p^* + (p_0 - p^*)\,e^{-k(b+d)t},
  \qquad
  p^* = \frac{a+c}{b+d}.
\]
Convergence: $k(b+d) > 0$ always.

methodology.title: Solving the Price ODE via Integrating Factor

modules.title: The Lotka--Volterra Predator-Prey Model
modules.topics: The Lotka--Volterra system produces closed phase-plane orbits guaranteed by the first integral $H(x,y)$.

formulas.title: Lotka--Volterra System and Conserved Quantity
formulas.body:
$\dot{x} = ax - bxy$, $\dot{y} = -cy + dxy$ ($a,b,c,d > 0$):
\[
  E_0 = (0,0), \qquad E^* = (c/d,\; a/b).
\]
\[
  H(x,y) = dx - c\ln x + by - a\ln y = \text{const} \quad
  (\text{minimum at } E^*).
\]

methodology.title: Deriving the Conserved Quantity
steps: Rearrange: $H = dx - c\ln x + by - a\ln y$.
Verify $\nabla H = 0$ at $E^*$ and Hessian positive definite.

warning.title: Common Pitfall: The Coexistence Equilibrium Is a Center
warning.body:
$(c/d, a/b)$ is a \emph{center} (closed orbits), not a stable spiral.
Perturbations do not damp out in the pure Lotka--Volterra model; adding
saturation converts the center to a stable spiral.

modules.title: Competition Models
modules.topics: Two-species competition: the outcome depends on the relative positions of the $x$- and $y$-nullclines.

formulas.title: Competition System and Interior Equilibrium
formulas.body:
$\dot{x} = r_1 x(1 - (x + \alpha y)/K_1)$,\;
$\dot{y} = r_2 y(1 - (y + \beta x)/K_2)$.
\[
  E_3 = (x^*, y^*), \quad
  x^* = \frac{K_1 - \alpha K_2}{1 - \alpha\beta}, \quad
  y^* = \frac{K_2 - \beta K_1}{1 - \alpha\beta}.
\]
Nullclines: $x + \alpha y = K_1$ and $\beta x + y = K_2$.

methodology.title: Coexistence vs.\ Exclusion: Nullcline Intercept Test

concept.title: Unit 8 Key Takeaway --- Part I
concept.body:
Clairaut equations split into a \highlight{general line family
$y = Cx + f(C)$} and a \highlight{singular envelope} from
$x + f'(p) = 0$. The Poiseuille profile
\highlight{$u = (G/2\mu)\,y(h-y)$} and price path
\highlight{$p(t) = p^* + (p_0 - p^*)e^{-k(b+d)t}$} follow from
linear BVPs. Lotka--Volterra orbits are level sets of
\highlight{$H = dx - c\ln x + by - a\ln y$}; competition outcomes
follow from \highlight{$\alpha\beta < 1$} (coexistence) versus
\highlight{$\alpha\beta > 1$} (exclusion).

warning.title: Common Pitfall: Interior Equilibrium Feasibility
warning.body:
$E_3$ is biologically meaningful only when both $x^* > 0$ and $y^* > 0$.
Check the signs of numerators and denominator before drawing conclusions.

cheat.subtitle: Condensed Cheat Sheet: Special Equations and Classical Models

takeaway.title: Unit 8 Key Takeaway
takeaway.body:
Every Clairaut equation $y = xy' + f(y')$ yields a
\highlight{general line family $y = Cx + f(C)$} and a
\highlight{singular envelope} obtained by eliminating $p$ from
$x + f'(p) = 0$. Pursuit geometry converts tangency and speed constraints
into separable second-order ODEs. The Poiseuille profile
\highlight{$u = (G/2\mu)\,y(h-y)$} and the price path
\highlight{$p(t) = p^* + (p_0 - p^*)e^{-k(b+d)t}$} both follow
from linear BVPs. In Lotka--Volterra dynamics the
\highlight{conserved quantity $H = dx - c\ln x + by - a\ln y$}
organizes all phase-plane orbits as closed curves around the coexistence
equilibrium $(c/d, a/b)$; in two-species competition the entire outcome is
decided by \highlight{$\alpha\beta < 1$ (stable coexistence) versus
$\alpha\beta > 1$ (competitive exclusion)}.
